root_cause.text: The built-in URLValidator was vulnerable to a regular expression denial of service (ReDoS) due to catastrophic backtracking when processing un-trimmed URL strings containing specific crafted patterns.
root_cause.evidence: The patch in `URLValidator.java` adds a `.trim()` call to the input value before matching it against the URL regular expression (`matcher(String.valueOf(value).trim()).matches()`), preventing the excessive processing time.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The built-in URLValidator was vulnerable to a regular expression denial of service (ReDoS) due to catastrophic backtracking when processing un-trimmed URL strings containing specific crafted patterns.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch in `URLValidator.java` adds a `.trim()` call to the input value before matching it against the URL regular expression (`matcher(String.valueOf(value).trim()).matches()`), preventing the excessive processing time.

intent_record.intent_summary: Trim whitespace from the URL string before regex validation to prevent false validation failures.
intent_record.what: Added a .trim() call to the string value before applying the regular expression matcher.
intent_record.how: Removes leading and trailing whitespace from the input string, ensuring that URLs with accidental surrounding spaces are not incorrectly invalidated.

grouping_result.intent: Trim whitespace from the URL string before regex validation to prevent false validation failures.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by trimming the URL string before regex validation, which removes the condition (un-trimmed URL strings) that triggers the catastrophic backtracking and ReDoS.

candidates.rationale: This line compiles the regex and matches it directly against the untrimmed value, leading to the ReDoS vulnerability.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement compiles the URL regex and matches it against the un-trimmed value string. Because the string is untrimmed, specially crafted input can trigger catastrophic backtracking in the regex matcher, causing a ReDoS.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit d19b9eaa82753a5dd671ee4f0847574f65f4aebf
Author: Lukasz Lenart <lukaszlenart@apache.org>
Date:   Thu Jul 28 13:09:21 2016 +0200

    WW-4671 makes URLValidator case insensitive

diff --git a/core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java b/core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
index fd678a70b..735d861de 100644
--- a/core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
+++ b/core/src
bic_trace_step.reason: The code uses Pattern.compile(getUrlRegex(), Pattern.CASE_INSENSITIVE).matcher(String.valueOf(value)).matches() to validate the URL. It does not trim the input string before validation, leaving it vulnerable to the regular expression denial of service (ReDoS) root cause.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit c50605a945b7b6b62b0869210a3146efd084b2ae
Author: Lukasz Lenart <lukaszlenart@apache.org>
Date:   Tue Jun 23 12:11:49 2015 +0200

    WW-4518 Drops another deprecated APIs

diff --git a/core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java b/core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
index 767416deb..f49d91468 100644
--- a/core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
+++ b/core/src/main/
bic_trace_step.reason: The function evaluates the regular expression against the raw value `(String) value` without trimming it first. Since the root cause of the ReDoS vulnerability relies on processing un-trimmed URL strings, this code is vulnerable and does not contain the fix.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Extends validator to allow set predefined regex used to validate URLs
bic_trace_step.reason: The snippet introduces a complex regular expression in the `getUrlRegex()` method (lines 100-113) that contains nested and overlapping quantifiers (e.g., `([a-z0-9]\.|[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]\.)*`), which is the root cause of the catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) vulnerability. There is no trimming or mechanism to prevent the backtracking, meaning the fix is not present.
